greeting: Hi! I'm your onboarding co-pilot. Ask me anything about this repo and I'll point you to the right place.
Hey there — welcome aboard! I can summarize files, explain flows, or suggest a learning path. What do you want to do first?
fallback:
I don't have a confident answer yet, but here's what I'd check next:

• The architecture diagram for component responsibilities.
• The folder structure for where this concern usually lives.
• Ping the assigned SME for a quick 10-min walkthrough.
That's a good question. Based on the repo signals I see, I'd recommend reading the on-call runbook and the recent ADRs. Want me to open the Knowledge Hub filtered to your topic?

responses:
Here's a quick summary I generated:

• Purpose: provides a reliable surface for downstream consumers.
• Stack: TypeScript / Node.js / PostgreSQL / Redis.
• Boundaries: thin controllers, rich domain, infra at the edges.
• Deploy: GitHub Actions → Argo CD with canary rollouts.

Want me to drill into any of these?

Authentication flow:

1. Client hits the API Gateway over HTTPS.
2. Gateway verifies the JWT against the Identity service's public key.
3. Verified user context is forwarded to the downstream service as signed headers.
4. Per-route authorization is checked in the controller middleware.

The JWT_PUBLIC_KEY env var must match the current signing key — that's the #1 source of 401s here.

Deployment pipeline:

• Build & sign image in CI (GitHub Actions).
• Scan dependencies + SBOM (Trivy + CodeQL).
• Auto-deploy to staging on merge to main.
• Canary 5% with SLO-based auto-rollback (Argo Rollouts).
• Promote to production when canary is green for 15 min.

The full diagram is in the Deployment section of the artifact.

The most common build failures I've seen here:

• Port already in use → kill the prior process or change PORT.
• JWT verification failing → rotate JWT_PUBLIC_KEY from the secrets manager.
• Slow DB queries → check the (tenant_id, created_at) composite index.

Want me to open the Common Issues page?

Welcome! Here's the path I'd suggest for your first 2 days:

1. Run the service locally and hit /v1/health.
2. Read the architecture diagram + glossary.
3. Trace one request end-to-end in code.
4. Pick up a ‘good first issue’ — your buddy will review.

Open the Learning Path page to check items off as you go.

At a high level: Client → API Gateway → (Identity for auth, this service for domain logic) → Postgres for state, Redis for hot caches, Event Bus for async. The diagram is interactive on the Architecture tab.

Three layers of tests live here:

• Unit — pure domain logic, fast, runs on every save.
• Integration — spins up real Postgres + Redis via docker-compose.
• E2E — black-box against a deployed environment; runs nightly.

Unit + integration must pass to merge.

For this repo, your go-to contacts are:

• Priya Mehta — Tech Lead (architecture, k8s)
• Marco Bianchi — Backend SME (API design, postgres)
• Devon Carter — DevOps SME (CI/CD, deploys)

See the Team page for the full escalation matrix.
